test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-zzzz-include.R b/tests/testthat/test-zzzz-include.R index ffd67655..5c4ef0c6 100644 --- a/tests/testthat/test-zzzz-include.R +++ b/tests/testthat/test-zzzz-include.R @@ -26,12 +26,18 @@ test_that("Includes work", { if (rmarkdown::pandoc_available()) { res <- PlumberResponse$new() val <- r$route(make_req("GET", "/md"), res) - expect_match(val$body, "R Output.*\\s*$") + expect_match(val$body, "

R Output

") expect_equal(val$headers$`Content-Type`, "text/html; charset=UTF-8") res <- PlumberResponse$new() val <- r$route(make_req("GET", "/rmd"), res) - expect_match(val$body, "\\s*$") + + # Ex test output received from CRAN + test <- '

]* src=\"data:image/png;base64") + + # Make sure image shows up in response + expect_match(val$body, "]* src=\"data:image/png;base64") expect_equal(val$headers$`Content-Type`, "text/html; charset=UTF-8") } })

fail_to_pass: test_that('Includes work') - the regex should match img tags with base64 src regardless of additional HTML attributes
pass_to_pass: Other tests in test-zzzz-include.R continue to pass

key_changes: Replace overly specific regex pattern with a more flexible one
Use pattern like '<img[^>]* src="data:image/png;base64' to match img tag followed by any attributes before the src attribute
Remove the requirement for specific HTML structure (opening/closing html tags) that may vary
potential_pitfalls: Making the regex too permissive could match false positives
Need to ensure the pattern still validates that base64 image data is present
Should test the regex pattern against known CRAN output before applying to actual response

instruction: Fix a failing test in test-zzzz-include.R that is too brittle and fails when HTML output format changes. The test checks that R Markdown includes render properly with embedded base64 images. The regex pattern for matching the tag needs to be more robust to handle variations in HTML attributes (like 'aria-label' being added). Update the test to use a flexible regex that matches the img tag with base64 image source regardless of what other attributes are present.
context: Test file: tests/testthat/test-zzzz-include.R Original failing test: expect_match(val$body, "\\s*$") The test was failing on CRAN because pandoc/rmarkdown added an 'aria-label' attribute to the img tag, breaking the specific regex pattern. The HTML output now looks like:
